description_long: This object is a large, olive-drab bomb with a rounded, bulbous main body and a prominent square tail fin assembly. The surface is metallic, featuring visible panel lines, rivets, and several latch-like structures distributed symmetrically around the body. The tail section is boxy and reinforced, with cross-bracing and a central circular opening, likely for stabilization during flight. The nose is rounded and smooth, with no visible fuse or protruding elements, while the tail end is more complex and industrial in appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century aerial bombs, such as the 'Fat Man' atomic bomb, and it is constructed primarily from metal with a utilitarian, military finish.
description: A large, olive-green metallic bomb with a rounded body and a reinforced square tail fin assembly, featuring visible rivets and panel lines.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bomb, rounded nose, and the square tail fin assembly with visible panel lines and latches.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the square tail fin structure with cross-bracing and a central circular opening.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the bomb's rounded body and tail assembly, with panel details and latches.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the rounded nose and the symmetrical arrangement of latches and panel lines around the body.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: No visible external detonation mechanism; detonation is likely initiated internally or via a concealed fuse or triggering device.
